layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: SINGLE-CONTAINER FORMAL TABLE LAYOUT. The slide uses a top-to-bottom orientation with the title at the top and a single full-width table container filling the remaining space. The table is structured as a formal regulatory/legal data table using the Flat Geometry design pattern (Section 4 Option C) — no filled header bar, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey label cells in the first column, and thin dashed grey separator lines between rows. The layout maximizes vertical space utilization while maintaining generous padding appropriate for the sparse content density (4 data rows). The table spans approximately 85% of the slide width, horizontally centered, with equal left and right margins. Vertical distribution is balanced: title occupies the top 12% of slide height, a visible gap separates title from content, and the table container fills the remaining space with internal padding that allows content to breathe without feeling cramped. All column edges align to a 4-column grid structure. Dashed separator lines provide visual rhythm between rows without consuming excessive vertical space.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.88]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH FORMAL TABLE): This container holds a 4-column × 5-row formal regulatory table displaying tax categories for virtual asset activities. CONTENT SUMMARY: Column headers are 'Tax Type', 'Rate', 'Applicable Activity', 'Legal Basis'. Four data rows contain: Income Tax (10-35%), Capital Gains (15%), Excise Duty (10%), and Withholding Tax (Various), each with corresponding activity descriptions and legal references. SPATIAL RELATIONSHIP: Container is horizontally centered on the slide, spanning approximately 85% of slide width. Top edge sits below the title with a visible gap (approximately 3-4% of slide height). Container fills remaining vertical space down to the slide's bottom safe-area margin. PROPORTIONAL WEIGHT: Height approximately 70% of slide height, width 85% of slide width. DESIGN INTENT: Apply Flat Geometry Table pattern (Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C) — this is formal/regulatory content requiring the flat aesthetic. Column headers are bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned within each column, with no filled background. Directly beneath each header text, place a small accent tab (brand primary color fill, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching each accent tab, draw a thin solid horizontal line in brand dark color spanning that column's width only (with small white gutters between columns — not a single full-width line). First column (Tax Type) contains row label cells: light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners) spanning most of the row height, with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered. Other three columns contain plain text on white background (no fill), body font in brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered to align with the label cell baseline. Between each data row, place a thin dashed grey horizontal separator line spanning the full table width (all four columns). No separator after the last row. All row sections use equal flexGrow values so rows align horizontally across all columns. Column widths: Tax Type column sized to fit longest label text without wrapping (approximately 20% of table width), Rate column narrow (approximately 12% of table width), Applicable Activity column widest (approximately 40% of table width), Legal Basis column moderate (approximately 28% of table width). Internal padding: generous on all sides — content must not crowd the container edges. Font size: body font at standard size, sufficient to ensure all text fits comfortably within cells without wrapping in the Applicable Activity and Legal Basis columns. SPECIAL NOTES: This is formal regulatory content (tax rates, legal references) — the Flat Geometry pattern (Option C) is mandatory per Rule J2. Do NOT use a filled header bar even though the source slide has one. The accent tabs and solid lines beneath headers provide the visual hierarchy. All row label cells must have identical grey fill and styling. Dashed separator lines are required between rows to create visual rhythm (Rule 0.10). The container has no outer border or background fill — it sits directly on the white slide background. All column edges must align precisely across rows (Rule B2). The table must feel balanced and professional, with whitespace used purposefully to frame the content rather than appearing as accidental gaps.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide content (single table) fills the available space appropriately. No bottom decorative layer is needed — adding one would violate the accent color budget and provide no visual grouping benefit. The table sits directly on the white slide background.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains a single table with no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. No top-layer execution is required.
